machinery_genes.quote: VirB1 carries a trans-glycosylase motif and is thought to 'punch' holes in the peptidoglycan to facilitate T4SS assembly.

machinery_genes.role: major pilin subunit of the T pilus / secretion channel component
machinery_genes.quote: VirB2 is the major pilin subunit of the A. tumefaciens VirB/D4 T pilus and an essential component of the secretion channel.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane subunit, pilus assembly / substrate transfer
machinery_genes.quote: VirB3 and VirB5 are required for substrate transfer.
VirB4 subunits are large inner membrane proteins with consensus Walker A and B nucleoside triphosphate-binding domains.
VirB5 subunits are exported to the periplasm, and they also localize extracellularly as components of the pilus.
The VirB6 subunits are highly hydrophobic with multiple predicted TMS.
VirB9 assembles as a disulfide cross-linked heterodimer with the VirB7 lipoprotein.
VirB8 and VirB10 are both bitopic IM proteins.
The VirB9 subunits are generally hydrophilic and predicted to localize in the periplasm or outer membrane.
VirB10 proteins function as structural scaffolds.
VirB11 is a member of a large family of AT-Pases associated with systems dedicated to secretion.
VirD4 codes for the coupling protein (CP).

notes: Full VirB1-VirB11 + VirD4 core curated from two canonical Christie reviews of the Agrobacterium VirB/D4 T4SS (the field archetype). Umbrella anchor from Christie 2004 (BBA, PMC4845649): 'The virB operon encodes 11 genes, virB1 through virB11'; VirD4 is encoded by the separate virD operon and is the coupling protein. Reviews describe the A. tumefaciens VirB/D4 system generically; much foundational genetic/biochemical work used the octopine-type Ti plasmid pTiA6 (strain A348) as well as C58. For this instance the virB operon resides on Ti plasmid pTiC58 = NC_003065.3. virD4 also lies on pTiC58 (virD operon). Effectors (Atu6154/6182/6188/6190, VirE2/E3/F/D5) and T-DNA processing genes (VirD2 relaxase, VirC1/2) are excluded per apparatus rules. VirB1 lytic transglycosylase and VirD4 coupling protein included as apparatus. Per-component quotes drawn from whichever of the two reviews phrased the role most explicitly; both reviews jointly establish the complete 12-component apparatus set.
